generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element that generates and maintains temperature within the styrofoam enclosure, controlled via Raspberry Pi's embedded logic to achieve precise thermal regulation for biomedical applications."
]

instance IncubatorFan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical fan component responsible for uniform air circulation within the styrofoam enclosure to prevent thermal stratification and ensure consistent temperature distribution throughout the incubator volume."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer deployed within the incubator enclosure, providing real-time temperature measurements with ±0.5°C accuracy for internal environmental monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer deployed within the incubator enclosure, providing real-time temperature measurements with ±0.5°C accuracy for internal environmental monitoring at a secondary spatial location."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance AmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External DS18S20 temperature sensor monitoring ambient environmental conditions outside the incubator enclosure to account for external thermal influences on the system's performance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance AmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AmbientTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based differential equation model representing the incubator's thermal dynamics, incorporating heat transfer principles and calibrated with real-time sensor data to predict temperature distributions within the enclosure."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalCalibrationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model accounting for external environmental factors (e.g., ambient temperature, humidity) that influence the incubator's thermal performance, integrated with the thermal dynamics model for comprehensive system representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalCalibrationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series dataset combining processed temperature readings from all internal and external sensors, synchronized with the Physical Twin's operational timeline for model calibration and state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, AmbientTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance CalibrationParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Parameter estimates derived from calibration algorithms, including heatbed efficiency coefficients, fan performance metrics, and thermal mass properties, used to refine the physics-based models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelRefinementEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B embedded system executing control algorithms, interfacing with sensors via GPIO, and managing bidirectional communication with the Digital Twin through RabbitMQ for real-time system monitoring and actuation."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataAcquisitionService, ControlAlgorithmExecutionService
]

instance ThermalCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based calibration framework utilizing SciPy's differential equation solvers to iteratively adjust the thermal dynamics model parameters using least squares estimation techniques, ensuring model fidelity with real-world sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicModelCalibrationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance EnvironmentalCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Environmental compensation module implementing Bayesian parameter estimation to account for external factors (e.g., ambient temperature variations) in the Digital Twin's predictive models, reducing prediction errors under varying operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnvironmentalModelingService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data processing pipeline implementing Kalman filter-based sensor fusion to reconcile discrepancies between multiple temperature sensors, generating a unified state estimate for the incubator's thermal environment."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]

instance ModelRefinementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Parameter optimization engine employing gradient descent algorithms to refine model parameters (e.g., heat transfer coefficients) based on calibration metrics, minimizing prediction errors and improving simulation accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelOptimizationService
]

instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker co-located on the Raspberry Pi facilitating asynchronous communication between physical sensors, the Digital Twin's enablers, and external monitoring systems, ensuring reliable data transmission with periodic polling intervals."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataAcquisitionService
]

// C6: Services
instance SensorDataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Periodic polling service collecting temperature measurements from DS18S20 sensors via Raspberry Pi's GPIO interface, transmitting raw data to the Digital Twin through RabbitMQ for real-time state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStateInsight, DataQualityAlert
]

instance DynamicModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous calibration service updating the thermal dynamics model parameters using real-time sensor data and differential equation solvers, ensuring the Digital Twin's predictive accuracy aligns with the Physical Twin's observed behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, CalibrationStatusAlert
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection service comparing model predictions with sensor readings to identify deviations (e.g., sensor drift, heating element failure), triggering alerts when performance metrics exceed predefined thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDiagnosisInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Sensor fusion service implementing Kalman filters to generate a unified state estimate of the incubator's thermal environment from multiple sensor inputs, providing a single consistent representation for decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides CurrentStateInsight
]

instance EnvironmentalModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Environmental compensation service integrating external temperature and humidity data into the Digital Twin's models to account for ambient conditions, improving predictive accuracy under varying operational scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalImpactInsight
]

instance ModelOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Parameter optimization service manipulating model parameters (e.g., heatbed resistance, fan efficiency) to minimize deviations from desired thermal states, improving energy efficiency and operational performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance ControlAlgorithmExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supervisory control service receiving state estimates from the Digital Twin to adjust the Physical Twin's actuators (e.g., heatbed, fan) via Raspberry Pi's embedded logic, maintaining desired operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time aggregated temperature state of the incubator environment derived from sensor fusion, providing engineers with current operational conditions for monitoring and decision-making."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measure of the Digital Twin's model accuracy, calculated from calibration metrics comparing predicted vs. observed temperature behaviors, used to assess model reliability."
]

instance FaultDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Diagnostic analysis identifying potential faults in the Physical Twin (e.g., sensor drift, actuator failure) based on deviations between model predictions and sensor readings, enabling proactive maintenance."
]

instance CurrentStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified state estimate of the incubator's thermal environment combining data from all sensors, providing a single consistent representation for real-time monitoring and control."
]

instance EnvironmentalImpactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of external environmental factors (e.g., ambient temperature variations) on the incubator's performance, highlighting potential operational challenges and mitigation strategies."
]

instance DataQualityAlert : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alert mechanism flagging potential data quality issues (e.g., sensor outliers, transmission errors) in the Digital Twin's data pipeline, ensuring reliable decision-making processes."
]

instance CalibrationStatusAlert : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Notification of the current calibration status, including parameter adjustment frequencies and model accuracy trends, used to monitor the Digital Twin's ongoing refinement process."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated maintenance suggestions generated from fault detection analysis, recommending specific actions (e.g., sensor recalibration, actuator replacement) to restore system performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Supervisory control commands transmitted from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin's Raspberry Pi controller, adjusting heatbed and fan parameters to maintain desired thermal conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Parameter adjustment suggestions generated by the optimization service to improve energy efficiency and thermal stability, requiring human validation before implementation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C18: Environments/Components
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Thermal environment encompassing the styrofoam enclosure, heatbed, fan, and all internal components where temperature control is maintained for biomedical applications."
    base:contains ThermalInsulationBox, HeatbedActuator, IncubatorFan
]

instance ThermalInsulationBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam enclosure providing thermal insulation to minimize heat loss/gain, maintaining the incubator's controlled environment with high precision for sensitive biological samples."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance PhysicalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Complete Physical Twin system integrating all hardware components (sensors, actuators, controller) and their operational environment, serving as the real-world counterpart to the Digital Twin."
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, AmbientTemperatureSensor, HeatbedActuator, IncubatorFan, RaspberryPiController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **bang-bang controller** to generate control commands for the Physical Twin, transmitting commands via the same **RabbitMQ** message broker used for data ingestion. Command formats are not explicitly detailed, but the system includes **human-in-the-loop validation** for critical adjustments. Feedback mechanisms are implicit through the **monitoring and calibration algorithms** that detect performance deviations (e.g., temperature deviations) and trigger corrective actions. No explicit conflict resolution, authentication, or command execution confirmation mechanisms are described beyond the controller’s binary output (on/off)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates within a closed-loop temporal framework where **data acquisition** from the Physical Twin (PT) is periodically executed by low-level drivers (e.g., temperature sensors) to feed real-time operational data into the Digital Twin. The **calibration algorithms** periodically update the system models (e.g., Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environmental models) using the latest sensor readings, though explicit temporal frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds) are not specified in the provided documents. The **monitoring phase**—which checks for safe and optimal performance—relies on this updated data, with violations triggering immediate human or algorithmic intervention. Latency requirements are implied to be **minimal** to ensure real-time responsiveness, though no quantitative thresholds (e.g., sub-second delays) are defined. Temporal granularity is dictated by the **periodicity of sensor readings** and the **frequency of model recalibration**, both of which are critical for maintaining synchronization between the PT and DT. The system lacks explicit mention of asynchronous or event-driven time scales, focusing instead on a **synchronous, periodic update cycle** tied to sensor-driven data ingestion and model refinement."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT system follows a **modular architecture pattern** where core components—**data collection, models, and algorithms**—are tightly integrated to enable end-to-end functionality. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to refine models of the CPS and environment, followed by **monitoring services** that validate system performance against safety and optimality criteria. Resource management is implicit, with algorithms dynamically manipulating data and models to generate insights (e.g., fault detection, state estimation). System-wide coordination is achieved through **closed-loop feedback mechanisms**, where violations trigger human or automated interventions (e.g., parameter adjustments, reconfigurations). No explicit mention of orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or middleware (e.g., MQTT, Kafka) is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  The document outlines key aspects of the Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle, though not explicitly segmented into formal stages. Key inferred phases and characteristics include:

  1. **Development Phase**:
     - **Representation Types**: The lifecycle begins with the creation of abstract models (e.g., CAD models for physical design, dynamic models for behavior analysis) to understand specific aspects of the Physical Twin (PT).
     - **Integration**: Models are not discarded post-deployment but integrated into the DT framework, combining **data** (sensor-collected), **models** (knowledge of PT and environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, optimizers).
     - **Transition Management**: The transition from standalone models to DT involves embedding them into a unified framework that supports real-time monitoring, simulation, and optimization.

  2. **Operational Phase**:
     - **Dynamic Updates**: The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with real-time data (e.g., sensor inputs) to refine parameter estimates of the PT and its environment.
     - **Monitoring and Control**: Monitors assess system performance (safety/optimality). Violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions (e.g., adjusting the PT).
     - **Lifecycle Governance**: Implicit governance involves iterative feedback loops between the DT and PT, ensuring alignment between virtual and physical systems. The document emphasizes the DT's role in **adding value** to the PT, suggesting continuous refinement and adaptation.

  **Not explicitly detailed**: Formal governance frameworks (e.g., roles, compliance), phase-specific milestones, or decommissioning processes.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The fidelity and validity of the Digital Twin system are ensured through a **calibration methodology** using a **non-linear least squares solver**, which minimizes residual errors between simulated and experimental data trajectories. The **validation process** involves comparing the resulting model behavior with real-time data from the Physical Twin (PT) to assess predictive accuracy. Uncertainty quantification is implicitly addressed through the calibration process, though no explicit statistical analysis of sensor noise or periodic recalibration protocols is mentioned. The **quality assurance** is primarily focused on the initial calibration experiment to achieve minimal residual error, with no detailed descriptions of ongoing error detection, correction mechanisms, or threshold-based alerts (e.g., ±1 °C deviations) provided in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical connection relies on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** as the primary node, utilizing **Wi-Fi and Bluetooth** for communication with the Physical Twin. The system employs **RabbitMQ** as the message broker for both data ingestion and command transmission, with no mention of additional protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus). Network topology is not detailed beyond the Pi’s local connectivity, and bandwidth/latency requirements are unspecified. Security protocols are not addressed; reliability and redundancy mechanisms are absent from the description."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed **locally on a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, with the server and message broker (RabbitMQ) running **on the same device**. No cloud/edge hybrid or on-premise infrastructure is mentioned; computational and storage requirements are not quantified. Scalability mechanisms are not discussed, and the deployment model is **single-node** with no redundancy or high-availability features. Availability/reliability assurance is not addressed beyond the system’s local operation."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure operational integrity of the Physical Twin (PT). Monitoring components within the DT continuously assess system performance to verify **safety and optimality**, detecting deviations from expected behavior. When violations are identified—such as unsafe or suboptimal conditions—the system triggers **corrective actions**, which may be automated or human-intervened. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to refine models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, ensuring parameter estimates align with real-time PT behavior. While explicit **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** are not detailed, the framework inherently relies on **monitoring-based safety checks** to mitigate risks associated with system failures or malfunctions. The DT’s architecture prioritizes **fail-safe mechanisms** by leveraging real-time data and model updates to prevent cascading failures or unsafe states in the PT."
]
